reviews:
[{'author_name': 'John Castillo Weddings', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/113033756163833141544/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWhdE4_IDntOa1_mp_jyQDoYgYwFPSfA7p7txMUGnxKwnNwiY4=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '4 months ago', 'text': "If you're looking for a wedding venue that embodies classic elegance and New York City sophistication, The Metropolitan Club is it. I recently had the pleasure of capturing a beautiful wedding at this historic landmark, and it was nothing short of extraordinary.

From the moment you step into its grand, gilded halls, you're greeted with jaw-dropping architecture and an atmosphere that feels like stepping back into the Gilded Age. Every detail, from the soaring ceilings to the intricate chandeliers, creates a stunning backdrop for wedding photos. The natural light that streams through the large windows makes it a dream for photographers like me.

Dress code is waived during check in and check out.', 'time': 1711430617, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Kareena Mehra', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/102666613836559526232/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKW-ViaLqzf5geW-N1-SCmnT0JoCQbFo5snM3eUgrSsy6PdE6I=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo', 'rating': 1, 'relative_time_description': '2 months ago', 'text': 'This is the worst hotel I’ve ever stayed in. For the amount of money I spent to be at NYAC, I felt segregated and attacked- my minority counterparts and I observed that we were the only ones being ridiculed for our clothes and being sent to the athletic entrance while our white counterparts were not. So many amenities, so many hidden gems, and great food!!!', 'time': 1639249376, 'translated': False}]

[{'author_name': 'John Ochenduski', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/109836176439232896905/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVkXKiV4yhQiaPXZYn9LIK7SEgji_AmkfBS3dGVtbFmms-7Ma2JVA=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba6', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a week ago', 'text': "This was my first time back in 40 years. Order many dark beers and enjoy yourself.", 'time': 1747868082, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Antonio Rybalch', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/100997947751460791308/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXkjK7IdaLOxPof-1mJJ8lzeJpj7iZ81TlDlNt59PN_ZdrSd3y_=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba5', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '2 months ago', 'text': 'Such a historic place with atmosphere of the last generation. Classic light and dark ale 🍺 Only cash

Some interesting facts

McSorley’s Old Ale House is the oldest Irish saloon in New York City, located at 15 East 7th Street in the East Village. Established in 1854, it is renowned for its historical significance and traditional atmosphere, featuring sawdust floors and Irish bartenders. The bar offers two types of ale: light and dark, and is famous for its cash-only policy. McSorley’s has a rich history, including being a “men-only” establishment until 1970, and has hosted notable figures like Abe Lincoln and John Lennon. It is a popular spot for both locals and tourists, offering a unique glimpse into “Olde New York”', 'time': 1742687730, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Jaime Boyer', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/115227119231724892119/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV01ZcuAh8rAWCA9xVtEJQ1CG6nxDSUztQIwnbSQzQW_2GB-EI=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba3', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '2 months ago', 'text': "This place has become a favorite for sure. It feels like stepping into a place where tradition meets quiet elegance. The menu isn’t overwhelming, but every option feels thoughtfully chosen, leaning into classic flavors with a refined touch.

I began with a baked onion stuffed with fontina cheese and caramelized onion—rich, savory, and deeply satisfying. It was the kind of starter that sets the tone for a memorable meal. The main course was a standout: delicate venison chops, perfectly cooked, served alongside a poached pear that added just the right amount of sweetness and contrast. The balance of flavors was extraordinary—earthy, gamey, subtly sweet.

Before the meal, I had a gin martini—nothing showy, just perfectly dry, clean, and expertly made. Sometimes simplicity is the highest form of sophistication, and this one nailed it.

To close, I was served a glass of dessert wine I hadn’t tried before. It was smooth and fragrant, with notes of ripe fruit and a gentle honeyed finish. It complemented the meal beautifully and left a lasting impression.

Service was attentive without being intrusive, and the ambiance encourages you to slow down and savor every bite. Highly recommended for those who appreciate classic cuisine done with heart and precision.', 'time': 1743038191, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Bianca Perez', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/108184834974539343698/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWRt6dMxBVEmt2_3_cJxGYjQoAtRXAMjM48rEAyoo8tehpd17oc=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba3', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a month ago', 'text': 'AMAZING outdoor seating. reviews:
[{'author_name': 'Ray Bolden', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/114202258204635183902/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJAKc6W_s1FuNIEAoID0wSL_wiaAohYjCyUWXsw1odKjRMOxw=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba4', 'rating': 3, 'relative_time_description': '2 months ago', 'text': 'NY Suprema Pizza in NYC is an interesting spot that seems to attract a crowd, likely due to its prime location. However, I think there are much better pizza options around the city that deserve more attention.

The restaurant itself doesn't quite hit the mark in terms of cleanliness. Having to ask for someone to come clean the table felt off, and that made me question the overall attention to detail in the service. On top of that, we were asked to leave after finishing our meal because, as the staff said, "other people want to sit." This gave off a bit of a rushed, impersonal vibe.

One thing I do appreciate, though, is their inclusion of vegan pizza options. While I personally didn't enjoy their vegan slices, it's always a positive to see restaurants offering something for a variety of diets. It’s great for those who are looking for vegan options, even if the flavor didn’t hit the mark for me.

Overall, I wouldn't consider NY Suprema Pizza a staple. It’s more of a convenient place to grab a slice before hopping on the train, but it’s definitely on the pricier side. Then again, that's the reality of NYC pizza prices. If you're in a rush or nearby, it could be worth stopping by, but if you're seeking out the best slice in the city, I’d recommend looking elsewhere.', 'time': 1743023572, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Benjamin Law', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/109695648276603267081/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWiurjYA2GiTfkt5djDjqyD0284CnYehNpPtkNW0CLtxG-xW7dBCw=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba5', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a month ago', 'text': "There's a bit more variety than other places, like the fig one. reviews:
[{'author_name': 'Ruby Chan', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/115245662646465670811/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW3LrrVwi-mDW5WQKHXOamURBu-eEORn8gD4ZzWQ3IMFrFb_ts=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba6', 'rating': 4, 'relative_time_description': 'a week ago', 'text': 'Known for its traditional Cantonese comfort food, Mee Sum serves up hearty meals with most dishes under $10. It’s a no-frills, nostalgic diner-style spot that locals have cherished for generations.

During my visit, Hing Jea, Mee Sum’s manager who has been working there for over 20 years, made everyone feel right at home. She warmly greeted customers like old friends. She even treated my boyfriend a free lemon ginger drink.

The restaurant is small, but they serve a variety of food like dim sum, congee, steamed rice, and zongzi.

We got their most famous toisan style salted chicken rice. The ginger scallion sauce was best enjoyed with rice. The chicken had many bones but it was still delicious.

The skin of the har gaw was a little bit dried out. I guessed their dim sum has been sitting in the steamer since early morning, as they open at 5 or 6 am every day.', 'time': 1747789875, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Juan Gutierrez', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/113989847163904010327/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJI_YUZ54slgqAMo0IBNN_eTe4dVuUHg3luFm7mu3a8uqB08A=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba7', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a year ago', 'text': 'This is old Chinatown! No frills lunch counter, cash only, delicious food. Came here for a Sunday comedy show which was great. During the week this is a regular breakfast and lunch counter where OG neighborhood folk come for authentic, cheap quick bites. They are embracing these special events now too to keep up with rising rents.

The sauteed cauliflower and the salted chicken were so incredibly delicious. The congee was extra tasty. Bao and dumplings were so good too. I need to come back again. Hope Mee Sum stays for a long time', 'time': 1708348191, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Louis (ahluu)', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/106445717288124551685/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVdx03wZVm7c_H9Z77HoOSbPKneoO5Su22uYGPolRn6G_m5tYvS=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba5', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a year ago', 'text': 'My second time patronizing them in new york city chinatown. [{'author_name': 'Gordon Slaven', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/112871205482979211217/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKhikPggdaYKZoPTyrNZ9yodxqrpOOYTwzvy3tHPopXrTqx4A=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba3', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '2 months ago', 'text': 'A great experience. I was recommended though so went for it and so glad that I did. It’s a beautiful set up with whole streets, amazing buildings including gardens which you see on a cute little tourist train and the ‘cathedrals’ are fascinating. There were a lot of people for the English tour which they managed really well by splitting into two groups. I was so very lucky to have Alejandro as a guide. He answered all questions patiently with really good knowledge. Often on tours you feel like the guide is reading a script and this was definitely not the case. It was well explained without being too basic but covered essentials. It is made really special with the history of the place and famous people signing barrels. My one big criticism is that there is one tasting area for all groups. It was far too noisy to hear the explanations of the wines tasted and the serving was rushed with no opportunity to see the bottles. That said, Alejandro took extra time with any questions and I didn’t feel at all rushed. A massive thanks and highly recommend a visit. I did the tour for 27 euros with 4 tastings which I recommend. The quality was excellent.', 'time': 1747292889, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Gareith Stanley', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/117397841016918699684/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU7M5cdNsSFmuI4omdwyGEEbnXBy-F8t57eoymq6nW7Y3wBZ54m=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba5', 'rating': 4, 'relative_time_description': '6 months ago', 'text': "The tours are excellent, friendly helpful guides. Suggest buying the 4 wine tasting as it's worth the money.
Our guide was amazing, however to be 5 stars they could reduce tour sizes (30 per group) so they could attend to the tasting better and be a bit less rushed.", 'time': 1731161495, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Tony Nicolas', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/115371860134973730360/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX51V7Hc08Y8TyCZkc27BSDQtCoyOXoGkVqc1XaJKh69_uGDdTN=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba4', 'rating': 4, 'relative_time_description': '11 months ago', 'text': "I don't understand why some people have given such negative reviews. I suspect they are just grumpy folk with a short fuse.
Our couple of hours were great.
Started off with a little train ride (actually bus pulling mini wagons, but still cute) around the grounds (a huge plot they own in the centre of town next to the cathedral) (I have given 4* instead of 5* as I would have preferred to stroll around the wonderfully ornate gardens on my own, at my own pace, but this isn't an option thus remove 1*).
Then a little walking tour through the old factory and storage rooms. Our guide was gentle and informative.
Ending up with the tasting of 4 different sherries. I would recommend the max tasting package. For the extra €5 you get two extra glasses of sherry, well worth it IMHO.
All in all, a great way to get gently pickled and taste some great drinks.", 'time': 1719503273, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'I B', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/103196399131732087035/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI3YudrQNJ5014cwUgnDceYkXxRmwSwGy7_R52QhyacQa8naQ=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba3', 'rating': 1, 'relative_time_description': 'a month ago', 'text': 'DO NOT waste your time on this bodega! Their wine tasting tour is a real scam and a tourist trap. First of all, if you are wondering, bodega is not a winery, it’s a wine storage place, so nothing interesting to see there, other than barrels with wine.
Secondly, we bought a 32euro/person package (most expensive one). For this price, they are promising 4 wine samples paired carefully with tapas. We expected a generous and delicious selection of tapas, but got none of that - only 2 small slices of cheese, 4 olives, peanuts and cheap crackers. Yes, that’s all you get for 64 euros! Not enough to even call it a snack. We were so mad after waiting 4 hours to do this tour. So basically, each 1-2 oz sample of wine is gonna cost you 8 euro. Please don’t waste your time visiting this place and take your money elsewhere!', 'time': 1745617921, 'translated': False}]

reviews:
[{'author_name': 'Chef Tommaso', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/104310350660779066471/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU9r7gSR7FOYsa2gzZ_W_wDevMVGHFiAuwo0JyZuMmiTfQqNG4=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba4', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a week ago', 'text': 'J.G. Melon is one of my favorite spots in NYC — a place from another time. Warm, charming, and impeccably clean. We absolutely love the burger. The tables are great, but sitting at the bar is a unique experience on its own. A true New York institution. The entire staff is professional and friendly. An absolute must for anyone looking to enjoy an unforgettable, authentic New York experience', 'time': 1747796579, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Garfield X', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/104758304501085459456/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJO9J4_8Os_PjQkJX7Kc9q6pmwfkgiNBuCS7c0BwZmQR0sTJQ=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba4', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '2 months ago', 'text': 'It’s almost like getting a bagel in New York — waiting in line is part of the ritual, building anticipation. The scent of sizzling patties and the sight of burgers constantly flying out of the kitchen send your brain into pre-meal overdrive.

We actually came here twice. The first time, we gave up — the wait was just too long. The second time, we got lucky and snagged a table after about 20 minutes. While waiting at the bar, I had time to take in the half-open kitchen and quirky décor. At least twenty thick patties were cooking at once, filling the air with that irresistible, savory aroma.

The melon-themed interior is full of character — think melon art, melon clocks, melon paintings. It feels like you’ve stepped into the eccentric collection room of a mischievous melon-obsessed boy. It’s weirdly charming and totally genuine.

Then there’s the burger — the undisputed star of the show. It’s minimalist: just meat, cheese, and bread. Pickles and onions are served on the side. No lettuce, no tomato, no fancy sauces — just pure, unadulterated confidence in the quality of their beef. And they’re right to be confident. The patty is perfectly cooked — juicy, with just the right balance of fat and meat, slightly crispy on the outside, and tender inside. The fries? Also excellent.

Despite how busy it was, the service stood out. No one rushed us to leave after we finished. In many popular spots, you can feel the pressure to free up your table — but not here.

When we stepped outside, a cheerful crowd was still waiting. Everyone was smiling for some reason. Eating at JG Melon feels like a little festival, and I’d definitely come back — just for the vibe.', 'time': 1742778404, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Luana Paiva', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/117630104170086684793/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXWf7dsdclGwmSL6InGanB_zSTHZuM8DuRisTDAJsnuQk-mW2o=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba5', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a month ago', 'text': "Don’t be fooled by the line at JG Melon; the wait time is minimal, especially during the week. However, if you have a large party, it might be challenging to find a seat since the restaurant is relatively small but cozy. It offers an authentic NYC vibe, and the service is fast and quite good. When it comes to food, you have to try the burger and their cottage fries—they're a must. The burger is juicy and well-seasoned, while the fries are perfectly crispy and not greasy. If you find yourself on the Upper East Side, this restaurant is a must-visit.", 'time': 1745268132, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Taka H', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/104852759796487428154/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXFc0r0Z41fcFes0IuCob3ayzGBR0uYrvWdLP0cypyeNVlRgCuo=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba6', 'rating': 3, 'relative_time_description': 'a month ago', 'text': 'Is it worth you wait?

definitely an old school vibe inside and out

[{'author_name': 'Luis Gustavo Lascarez Vargas', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/105750574288181472765/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKN0B54-5b5ZiL_p0lo8rbVPPx48mCFCMxJxCpSeMZMNxPx_w=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '2 weeks ago', 'text': 'Yesterday I revisited Cafe Riazor as I have done many times in the last 50 years!! They all know me very well. I might even be a rare pioneer. The ambiance is cozy & welcoming. The place is clean. The music is very nice. The food is spectacular. These facts explain why I tell David & Miriam that Riazor is my second home. Yesterday I visited with my younger sister Maribel & her husband Juan. Like myself, they were left speechless after eating these entries: House Paella & Octopus Salad (my 2 favorites), clams & shrimp, Mar & Tierra, house salads, Estrella 0.0 Beer from Spain & natural juices. I lived in NYC 30 years, in South Florida 16 years & presently I reside in Costa Rica. Anytime I get a chance I turn to NYC & the delicious aromas of Cafe Riazor & the smiles of David & Miriam keep calling me here….!! & I am humbly ready to comply!! From its beginnings as a cozy 9-table eatery, Café Riazor has grown while staying true to its roots. The Chef/Owner’s dedication shines through in every dish, with a menu that beautifully highlights the bold, rich flavors of Spain. Whether you’re enjoying their signature Camarones al Ajillo, savoring a plate of paella, or sipping on their incredible sangria, every bite is a journey to Spain.

And here’s a fun fact: even Sarah Jessica Parker from the iconic TV shows Sex and the City and And Just Like That frequented this spot. It’s a true testament to how beloved Café Riazor is in New York’s dining scene!

The service is top-notch, the ambiance is warm and inviting, and the food is nothing short of spectacular. Café Riazor has been a Chelsea staple for 50 years, and it’s clear why—they’ve mastered the art of creating unforgettable dining experiences.

My point was, I expected the server to be knowledgeable enough about everything the restaurant sells and if it is a question from a customer, the server shouldn't have an ego. If he/she didn't know they should ask first before assumption.

reviews:
[{'author_name': 'Michael Lee', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/117535772467995380769/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWi8DVf7x2ALcfTiKLXqRj-eK6PLASbZAW32LTUbEne4hQfTG9QkA=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba6', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '3 weeks ago', 'text': 'Wow, what an incredible experience at the legendary Ear Inn! 🤩  From the moment I stepped inside, I felt like I'd been transported to another era – it's pretty surreal to enjoy some food & drink in a space that's been serving patrons since 1817.
Biggest surprise? The food! The fried pork and chive dumplings were out of this world. Seriously, the best dumplings I've ever had. Crispy, flavorful, and perfectly paired with their dipping sauce and some incredibly "horseradishey" English mustard. I highly encourage double-dipping in both and washing it down with an ice cold draft beer.
The service was also top-notch. Colin was friendly & attentive and shared some fascinating stories. This place has a classic NYC charm that you just can't find in newer establishments. You can feel the history in the walls and see the relics they've accumulated over the years.
If you're looking for a truly authentic NYC experience with great food, cold drinks, and a fantastic vibe, make sure the Ear Inn is on your list.', 'time': 1747145158, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Otavio Luis Barbosa', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/114447835829665059009/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXOTruBs5y0BLySlepGmo3vW8w0dGFDts7JMYFdU344j62UGiEGgw=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba3', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a month ago', 'text': 'I had the best time there with my boyfriend. reviews:
[{'author_name': 'Conor McNamara', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/105838109311285526489/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWXEmzXoufPmX74zmN9aKCfvQcnBhjk8hQEHiTmHcKi2hmBbMdiJw=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba2', 'rating': 1, 'relative_time_description': 'a month ago', 'text': 'I never write reviews and as someone who’s been in industry for 15 years I loathe online review culture, but… Spare yourself. A very telling illustration of just how bleak the upper east side is. 38 dollars for an iced coffee, “fresh” oj and the worst little breakfast wrap I’ve had in my life. Literally tasted like cardboard- no seasoning and using grocery store style lunch wraps that felt stale. More bread than filling. The atmosphere is bleak and uncomfortable. The food carts in the park nearby have more worthwhile food that actually tastes like something.

Thank you Ty’s for keeping their memories alive!', 'time': 1712600502, 'translated': False}]

[{'author_name': 'Amber Gray-Kurz', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/110643775869217259420/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKsQPQKMnW9T3zWT568fo7n9rGpPFOCZ7hpr2T4b16xYdA2pQ=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba6', 'rating': 3, 'relative_time_description': '6 months ago', 'text': 'I stopped at this restaurant randomly while exploring the city. It had good reviews so I decided to give it a shot. Unfortunately, my experience was not entirely positive. It was a small restaurant but they were pretty busy. I only saw one waitress for the entire restaurant, including the outside seating. Another employee came up and spoke to the waitress while she was trying to work. I got the impression it may have been the manager. She continued to work and I could tell she got visibly annoyed as he kept talking and she was trying to work. I ordered the Birria Tacos and they were good, not great. I did appreciate they were served with three sauces. The green sauce was definitely my favorite. The other two were a bit underdeveloped.

The worst part of the experience was the end. I received the tacos but was not acknowledge after that. I finished and wait for the check. The waitress was busy but the other employee that had stopped her from working was standing around. 15 minutes after finishing my meal I was still waiting for my check. I got out my credit card hoping that would speed up the process. Another 5 minutes + later the employee that was standing around noticed and pulled the waitress aside and made eye contact with me. She came over directly after and took the card. opening_hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: 4:30 – 10:00 PM; Thursday: 4:30 – 10:00 PM; Friday: 4:30 – 10:00 PM; Saturday: 4:30 – 10:00 PM; Sunday: 4:30 – 8:00 PM
reviews:
[{'author_name': 'Scotty Watson', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/107988828590003014624/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVN3sus5wpJJn8ObgvbgVPu7AcVm1sRovFmGHzPSr-8KVSTFuId=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba4', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a month ago', 'text': 'Tonight we took a risk on a restaurant I have walked past, (and peeked in the window), many times. Chez Napoleon 365 W50th St quite close to 9th Ave.

Opened in 1960, it’s an old school French restaurant that is both charming and cheeky. They have a sign that reads along the lines of, “If you take a picture of your food we will give you a complementary roll of the eyes.”  They take their food VERY seriously while having a little fun with their own attitude.

The service is superb!  Unrushed, friendly with humor and charm.

My wife has asparagus soup & sole (just delightful) and I had a green salad and the cassoulet.  This is something I often make for my father around the holidays and theirs is FAR superior to my own.  I have a LOT to learn!

We finished the night with a soufflé with raspberry sauce.  I could’ve eaten a thousand of them!  Absolutely delightful.

No food pictures, because I didn’t wanna get in trouble!

We’ll be back!', 'time': 1743908718, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Esther Ko', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/102052218253025441441/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXkStrwXpEWvECCPpH-FUrIpL8rAwW_8nGRmijihVaZcO317M7v=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba4', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a year ago', 'text': 'Would give it a 4.5 if I had the option to because the food was a little too salty.

First off, this place is a gem. If you’re looking for a cozy, quiet place to enjoy some home cooked-style French meals, this is it!

We ordered the rabbit stew and sea bass which were excellent, although the stew was too salty for our taste. They give you tasty complementary bread, and the stew came with some green beans which I absolutely loved as a palate cleanser.

Their soufflé is a must-have! It’s expensive, but it’s something you can’t try anywhere else in the US. It looks enormous but is mostly air inside - 2 people can easily finish it so don’t be intimidated by the size like we were.

Reservations are a must here. The restaurant is tiny so you will most likely not have any luck with a walk-in.

Lastly, it’s worth mentioning the service was great. Ambiance: the restaurant is big but still gives off the cozy vibe. They have an upstairs and downstairs area for seating. I personally like the upstairs because it’s darker and has more of an intimate vibe. The downstairs area is well lit and creates a more friendly/big group vibe.

Drinks: They have a full bar and can make most cocktails. They also have a great wine selection that is fairly reasonably priced.

Food: When you sit down they give you fresh celery, carrot sticks, and olives. It’s a great starter. After you order they give you freshly baked, hot bread. It was so good and their olive oil was the perfect compliment.
As vegetarians, the pasta option didn’t appeal to us exactly and the restaurant was very accommodating. They made us our own versions and we very much appreciated that. The pasta sauce tasted super fresh. I think the last time I had such fresh pasta sauce was in Italy. Pasta was cooked perfectly. The seasoning was on point - it didn’t taste bland or over seasoned.
They gave us delicious Italian cookies for dessert.

They start you off with bread, and something I’ve never seen elsewhere - a plate of iced raw veggies (carrots, celery, radish). This was a refreshing touch. The Shrimp Alla Gene’s was a mighty tasty appetizer cooked in a creamy white wine sauce. You might want to save some of your bread to use to mop it up. The pastas are exactly what you’d expect - homemade, fresh, piping hot.

I have no complaints and only praise for Gene’s. The moment you walk in, you’re hit with the comforting aroma of sizzling bacon, fresh coffee, and something sweet baking in the back. It’s a sensory welcome that says, you’re in the right place.

The booths are plush, and the menu—oh, the menu—is a love letter to diner culture. I ordered the Andrew’s Special Burger, piled high with crispy onion rings and a smoky house sauce that I swear could fix a bad day. And let’s talk about the pancakes: fluffy, golden, with edges just crisp enough to hold the butter like a secret. One bite and I was a kid again, grinning with syrup on my face. What truly sets Andrew NYC Diner apart isn’t just the food—it’s the vibe. I didn’t just eat there—I felt part of something timeless. And as soon as I stepped out onto the sidewalk, I knew I’d be back. reviews:
[{'author_name': 'Thea Pham', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/100401807268413064243/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU7Zp9kBJis-pNYHApCSUgbZcJul7KX7FyfxPKMOugul1N7SV1AEA=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a week ago', 'text': 'Absolutely one of the most delicate and thoughtfully curated Omakase experiences in NYC, with a good price. The freshness is top-notch, especially the chutoro and uni, which melted slowly in my mouth. The meal ends perfectly with a light Japanese cheesecake topped with cherry jelly, served with Japanese hot tea (3 options). I was so immersed. Plus, the calm, spacious interior adds to the overall experience. A true hidden gem for those who appreciate subtlety and craft.', 'time': 1748052181, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Shelbyc', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/114268993240558753435/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUSYMFlq2hiMe7gfEGYu3dSqTdlf0n1zJ1jigTtlq61qxpzUnb5Tg=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba4', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '4 months ago', 'text': "Hasaki holds a special place in my heart. During the mid 90's my ex boss took me there so that we can talk about my raise which I don't remember if I got or not, but what was memorable was the food and atmosphere itself. I've taken those who I consider close friends to eat there. Up until last week, I haven't been back in 20 years or so?

The decor was different yet, the same owner,  and same inviting warm atmosphere with an updated interior. I went for the Omakase and was thoroughly pleased. Will definitely be going back more often now. Straight up, the food is excellent as is the service.

Hidden gem maybe that’s why it hasn’t gone viral, everyone is gatekeeping.', 'time': 1729922418, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Michael Bromley', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/104496537018845257892/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW2wuH97LKStV7vJs6-8_hOhfQEDBX1l4qkaRKpRxTzx_fIjA_Q=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '10 months ago', 'text': 'Hands down the best omakase I’ve been to in the city for the price point. 12 courses with a soup and two desserts for less than $100. Two friends from high school and I arrived in torrential rain and the host met us with towels at the door. Halfway through the meal we realized the chef had been watching us as we sat down and arranged our chopsticks and ginger based on our dominant hand. Little things like this made it clear how important our comfort was to the staff. Things like that go a long way. Intimate and warm setting with extremely attentive staff. I would like to return soon to try their al la carte options. Good beer and wine list, excellent sake selection.

The 12 course meal featured amazing fresh fish, several of which were new to me after going to many omakases in the city. The torched pieces were done seared delicately in a way that enhanced the fish. In the past, I have found that being too liberal with the torch can leave a butane aftertaste which was not the case at Hasaki. I was excited to visit Miss Lily’s 7A Café after seeing it featured on the Food Channel as the #1 best place to eat Jamaican Jerk Chicken. As a Jamaican living in New York, I had high expectations for an authentic experience. My husband and I made reservations, which required a $25 deposit.
When we arrived, I ordered the pepper shrimp as my appetizer and jerk chicken as my entrée, while my husband chose the oxtail. The presentation of the food was beautiful, but unfortunately, the taste did not match the appearance. The pepper shrimp was a complete disappointment—just five shrimp drenched in an overwhelming pepper sauce with no real flavor. The highly recommended jerk chicken was grilled well but lacked seasoning, and the sauce tasted like a watered-down imitation of real jerk sauce. My husband’s oxtail was also bland and lacked the rich, slow-cooked flavor you expect from a proper Jamaican dish.
There is no way the owner(s) of this restaurant are native Jamaicans, and the cooks are definitely not Jamaican. This is not authentic Jamaican food, and it doesn’t even come close. reviews:
[{'author_name': 'Conrad Lloyd', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/101083359892234791097/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWBM_7yPBpWe0IJrwPRhQdgM_YZnINvrJsopOBiTIq7kvUPXDhW=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba5', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a week ago', 'text': 'This was simply phenomenal. If you are a fan of seafood this is one of the best places I’ve ever been to. Immediately when I entered the building I was amazed by the atmosphere and how accommodating the staff were. My dad and I came here as we have follow Eric Ripert’s career for a long time. To finally sit in this restaurant and taste the food was nothing less than a dream come true. Each dish showed a mastery of both taste and texture. Not trying to sound pretentious but the whole tasting menu itself was put together brilliantly. Every dish played off of the last. This was a once in a lifetime experience for us that I would HIGHLY recommend you try too.', 'time': 1747870487, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Tom Zeien', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/114602764735632877901/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWL8jF29ez-GzGWlhOCQjyqHWHzE8KyCY7nJfOE_YTCSJqpedsv9w=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba5', 'rating': 4, 'relative_time_description': 'a month ago', 'text': 'First things first: food and drink were all delicious and I had a wonderful time here for dinner on a Thursday evening. We decided to go with the four course prixe fixe menu, but the amount of food was on the lighter side compared to similar places in my opinion. I wasn’t starving at the end, but I wasn’t as satisfied as I was expecting, especially for the price and 3-star status of the establishment.

Service was very sharp and attentive, though some of the staff did have a shorter air about them. Not necessarily a knock, but it does add to the old-school formal business-y atmosphere that I found interesting. There’s definitely a unique vibe, and depending on what you’re looking for you may either really enjoy it or dislike it; your mileage may vary. As someone visiting from out of town I enjoyed it. It felt like a part of New York City that’s older and you can only get in certain places today. For example, there was basically nobody without a suit jacket on—I haven’t seen such adherence to a dress code in today’s world anywhere else and in that way it was impressive—and it felt like almost everyone was there for some sort of business thing vs special celebrations like birthdays or anniversaries.

The interior design is also a bit old school feeling, which I was into, but some may feel it “needs an update” though I’m not sure I agree. There was something special about it.

Overall it is worth checking out, but if you’re looking for something specific or have strong preferences I recommend doing a little research before committing if you’re on the fence.', 'time': 1744992249, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Richard Nohe', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/108903918462480227322/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX0SYfKtwvF8O1l3S9z9gglkyerovfr5xfMv21sW7tOsHmW4KGEoQ=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba2', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a week ago', 'text': 'My wife and I enjoyed a very nice meal here last November, and I’m just getting around to posting this several months late. In any case, we had the tasting menu with wine pairing, and it was all excellent. The courses were reasonably portioned, and the pacing was just right. At the end we were full and enjoyed a brisk walk but not overly stuffed. The menu also fit together well and all the pairings were spot on. No meat — just garlic, onions, Parmesan cheese, olive oil, butter, salt, and pepper — simple ingredients that came together perfectly.

Next, we had the rigatoni bolognese. A hearty, comforting dish with a well-balanced blend of protein and acidity. The portion was generous and satisfying.

Finally, we tried the porcini mushroom ravioli. I’m not sure what the sauce was, but it struck a perfect balance — not too heavy, not too acidic, not overly creamy. The rich filling of the ravioli paired wonderfully with the sauce.

reviews:
[{'author_name': 'Domenika Nilaj', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/113316829892533850190/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLMQuW6u1qTr6GGLt74B-e7Y7ZHpikX9U2uE6QaH_RFtkhQzw=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'a week ago', 'text': 'An Absolute Gem – Always a Delight!

This restaurant has quickly become one of my favorite spots, and for good reason. From the moment you walk in, you’re greeted with a warm, welcoming atmosphere and attentive service that makes you feel right at home.

name: Peculier Pub
address: 145 Bleecker St, New York, NY 10012, USA
opening_hours: Monday: 4:00 PM – 1:00 AM; Tuesday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM; Wednesday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM; Thursday: 4:00 PM – 3:00 AM; Friday: 3:00 PM – 4:00 AM; Saturday: 1:00 PM – 4:00 AM; Sunday: 3:00 PM – 12:30 AM
reviews:
[{'author_name': 'Gene Hirschel', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/116794788624266903373/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXnxIMenMSiHXJuQlfRpd3w-Hfcml6Yk4lmdDnLd2fgnea4QHiIVQ=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba6', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '3 weeks ago', 'text': "Tucked away in Manhattan’s storied Greenwich Village, The Peculiar Pub has long been more than just a bar—it’s a surviving relic of New York City’s bohemian past, a cozy sanctuary of amber lights and dark brews and everything in between. While so many bars in the city reinvent themselves to chase trends, the Peculiar Pub remains defiant and endearingly itself.
Peculiar pub was first born in 1982 on West 4th by a couple who had previously worked in a deli that featured a number of different international beers. They certainly took that to the next level and focused on creating a pub dedicated to the brewer’s arts.  When it opened, Peculier was ahead of its time, boasting a beer list that was downright audacious for the era. You see, back then most bars were slinging the usual suspects: Bud, Miller, Coors, maybe a Heineken if they were feeling fancy.  But Peculier came out swinging with a selection of over 300 beers from around the globe, including hard-to-find imports like Tusker from Kenya and Beer Lao from Laos.  In a city that hadn’t yet caught the craft beer bug, this was nothing short of a foamy revolution! Thus my beloved Peculiar Pub gained a cult following.
In 1987 they moved to a larger venue further east, but they kept the same vibe and moved many of the wall decorations and their cult following followed. The eclectic lineup they offer reads like a passport of brewing history. Long before microbrews were buzzwords, this bar was serving Trappist ales, obscure German bocks, and curious Asian lagers to college students, poets, professors, and wayward souls of the Village.
Its ambiance is still a delightful contradiction: dimly lit, slightly grimy, with walls covered in vintage beer signs and an unmistakable late-1970s energy. The tables are scratched with decades of conversations and carvings; it isn’t slick or polished—it's authentic, preserved not by design, but by inertia.
Generations of NYU students have adopted it as their second living room. A place to argue about philosophy or music over a $6 Belgian strong ale, or to learn the hard way what 11% ABV tastes like after midnight. Many longtime patrons wax nostalgic about the days before the city sterilized much of its character. The Peculiar endures because it never tried to outgrow its soul.
In a city that’s constantly bulldozing the old to make room for the new, The Peculiar Pub stands as a beer-stained monument to the weird, the welcoming, and the wonderfully out-of-step. It’s not just a bar—it’s a neighborhood institution, a faded flag of Village counterculture still flying proudly in the foam of a 17oz Schneider Weisse.

With the background out of the way, I will first give a list of all the countries represented on their beer menu: Argentina, Australia, Austria, Barbados, Belgium, Brazil, Bulgaria, Canada, China, Costa Rica, Czech Republic, Denmark, Dominican Republic, England, France, Germany, Greece, Guatemala, Haiti, Holland, India, Italy, Japan, Jamaica, Korea, Mexico, Peru, Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Scotland, Spain, Sri Lanka, Sweden, Vietnam, Ukraine, USA – and the USA beers are further divided into State of origin.

In terms of food, you are getting the standard pub fare here with extremely reasonable prices. I mean, where else in NYC can you find a $10 burger worth eating? Food isn’t their specialty, but offerings will stick to the ribs and satisfy.

If you are looking to pop back to the 80s and see what the Village really looked like… this is an excellent way to do just that!", 'time': 1746660223, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Maddy Chong', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/104615949707928355845/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW3vaHVDYAFumbxkwuiTGXxFOFFR4lfQk-KJVsey_yAiJtLq27SAA=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba4', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': 'in the last week', 'text': 'such reasonable prices, tons of group seating, food is tasty, what more can you ask for!! third space 🫶🏼🫶🏼', 'time': 1748525825, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Tina V', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/108384016343461685182/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXyoQwlrP08x03aKxWWN8eIEgV6q5SBJOMd3_fxL_zOoeJLPbg=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '3 months ago', 'text': 'This place is a favorite of mine and my fiancés. The service is wonderful as well. My wife and I have started ordering meals to go after having a baby, and when we're running late, they somehow keep everything hot like it was just prepared.

The lumberjack was good about $17 or so.', 'time': 1740170365, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Murat Kaan', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/105195301150578748440/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVS877-dsHRntYhWMPJVQTn677FllXEosT7rF8VbZlFGlR8aVPY=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba4', 'rating': 4, 'relative_time_description': 'a month ago', 'text': "Visiting Tom’s Restaurant during my trip to New York was a surprisingly emotional experience. As a long-time fan of Seinfeld, standing in front of the iconic facade—unchanged since the show aired—immediately brought back memories of scenes and laughs from one of the most legendary sitcoms of all time. It felt surreal to be there in person after seeing it so many times on screen.

Beyond Seinfeld, Tom’s Restaurant holds another cultural significance—it’s famously referenced in Suzanne Vega’s song “Tom’s Diner”. Knowing that this humble little diner has inspired such pop culture moments made being there feel even more special.

Inside, the atmosphere is classic, no-frills New York diner: friendly staff, fast service, and a cozy, down-to-earth vibe. I had a simple breakfast—eggs, toast, and coffee—and it was just right. It’s not about gourmet food, it’s about the nostalgia and the feeling of being connected to something bigger than just a meal.

If you're visiting New York and have even the slightest appreciation for TV history or music, Tom’s Restaurant is absolutely worth the stop. It’s more than just a diner—it’s a piece of American pop culture that still stands strong.", 'time': 1744739238, 'translated': False}]

address: 389 8th Ave, New York, NY 10001, USA
opening_hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 AM; Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 AM; Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 AM; Thursday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 AM; Friday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 AM; Saturday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 AM; Sunday: 12:00 PM – 4:00 AM
reviews:
[{'author_name': 'Olivia Klein', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/108044073474911401703/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXd_MM9aKMhzaPvz9PiqY4Tvnd5qn90CdPv3grM0OI6QB-D68Xf=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo', 'rating': 1, 'relative_time_description': 'a month ago', 'text': 'TLDR: Racist and rude

I rarely feel compelled to leave a review somewhere, however my experience at Walter's was pretty horrifying.

Around 2:30AM I walk into the bar with my friend. We approach the bar to order a drink and notice the bartender is wearing a concert t-shirt from the show we had both just attended at MSG (my friend actually playing in the band). In an attempt to be nice we ask if he was also at the show and were met with a dismissive and uninterested grunt...

After ordering our drinks he asks to see our IDs. I present my NY ID, my partner shows his TN ID. After looking at it and bending it for a few moments, he says he cannot accept his ID and needs to see another form of validation since he "doesn't look like his photo." We try to laugh it off as my friend is over 30 and VERY clearly the person in the ID photo. I ask if they have a scanner to scan the ID and am told that wouldn't matter. His behavior is generally weird, not making eye contact or using complete sentences?

We are confused, seeing as we enjoyed a night at this bar 2 days prior and the vibes were totally fine. He adamantly asks him to show another type of identification with his name on it. We decide to stop wasting our time with this racist man who very clearly has targeted my partner because he is Black.

After reading other reviews about Walter's Bar, this unfortunately does not seem to be an isolated experience with this particular white, male, heavy set bartender, as there are multiple accounts of people being denied entry based on skin color left in the reviews. It's pathetic, vile, and racist.

The continued employment of this individual is absolutely disgusting. Walter's Bar is racist trash. Don't waste your time here.', 'time': 1745004416, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Leonardo Carvajal', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/113595511379739670645/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVbLjy-vLenriyfAGu-J67H6A7NyMK7L7tuGg_Fr33zKF-_E2E=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba3', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '3 months ago', 'text': 'Andrew & Nicki best bartenders ever, great beer selection. Lovely atmosphere', 'time': 1739264743, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Blandon', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/115349839188610091607/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXDL2iWZIEWfQcU0-SLtkuWoBbFK9sd1a2jPrOhrRwajNUR5yol=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo', 'rating': 1, 'relative_time_description': 'a month ago', 'text': 'I’m extremely disappointed and honestly shocked by how I was treated by bartender Andrew, a heavyset white man at this bar. He outright refused to accept my UK Biometric Residence Permit (BRP) as valid ID and insisted that only a U.S. document was acceptable. We tried to explain to him multiple times patiently that the BRP is an official government-issued ID in the UK, widely recognized and used internationally. It even shares the same format codes and security features as many national passports.

But he wouldn’t listen. He waved it off rudely and kept repeating “It has to be a U.S. document,” with a dismissive tone, like anything non-American wasn’t good enough. It felt humiliating and clearly discriminatory. Foreign passports aren’t U.S. documents either,  but they can be accepted,  so what kind of logic is that?

The whole experience felt lazy, rude, and frankly, racist. He didn’t want to engage, didn’t want to help, and just wanted to push us out. It’s 2025, how is this kind of behavior still happening? We had one turkey and one roast beef , they were both fantastic. Thanks Shobuj for helping us build our bagel.", 'time': 1748216735, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Kerry Alaric Cheeseboro', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/109466204351783537057/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVjqiJaqORXN99TBD2VeM8q8mwyUVYJqxRLMcEeiYTHBs3-ekLc=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba5', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '7 months ago', 'text': "Always anxious to enjoy my favorite NYC food staples when making it back to Gotham, and as such I surprised myself after nearly two weeks realizing that I hadn’t had a good bagel, which I usually enjoy with lox or whitefish salad.

When I had the realization I somehow also reflected on how long it had been since a had a proper knish.

*Light bulb moment!* What if I had a lox knish sandwich? I was sure that this sandwich had to already be a “thing” but found little evidence of this idea on the internet besides a recipe for a more hors d'oeuvre version of bite-sized mini knish topped with smoked salmon.

So at 5:30am this morning I headed over to nearby and highly revered Tal Bagels and deli counterman Sobuj, who admitted never making one before himself, expertly put together fresh, hand-sliced smoked salmon on a warm potato knish, adding cream cheese, mustard, and red onion.

Though a potato knish may not have as near the structural integrity as a bagel (and rightfully so) it still made for a very manageable—and very delicious—handful of a meal. The smooth, silky salmon aromatized with pleasant smokiness, with its streaks of tasty fat rendered easily from the warm of my grateful tongue. piquant mustard and crisp red onion rings helped balance the fish’s rich oiliness, and the cream cheese and soft potato of the knish were amply supportive in both texture and subtler flavors.

Sobuj exclaimed he couldn’t wait to try one himself, and I assured him he might enjoy very much, and as I plan to revisit Tal Bagels during my present stay, often for this same sandwich, I look forward to hearing his thoughts on this fulling and totally satisfying sandwich.", 'time': 1729790949, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Sheridan Lutes', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/106218057343728483190/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWh-mKvwSDmaO4yE6WwIXPNddGUsnuuxMDADbRuJtOCvXikKosO=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo', 'rating': 5, 'relative_time_description': '2 weeks ago', 'text': 'LOVE THIS PLACE! No rulls at all!', 'time': 1702221034, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'Diana G', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/118201412410191340314/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLhC5Bff8_RpjBzqnnBiIkr-iATJabQcClDDDpP-Dxi18Ra1A=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba5', 'rating': 2, 'relative_time_description': 'a year ago', 'text': 'I felt like a Times Square tourist… And not in a good way-The quality of the food was very good, but that is the only real positive I have for Scalitenella.  where to start?
should I start at the end when I received a phone call from the restaurant at 11:45 PM(!) accusing me of leaving without paying my bill. Here’s how it unfolded:
1. THE RESERVATION - reservation was for 7:30 PM but not seated until 8:30 PM. The restaurant is small and very full. Kudos to them but wasn’t great for us.
2. THE BAR WAIT - we were asked to wait at the bar, which is quite small, we were a party of three, and there was one chair available. So we squeezed in and took the one chair and two of us stood for an hour. The entire time we were there an older gentleman was giving us the stink eye. because he felt he was there before us when the hostess asked us to sit there.
…at this point, things are still salvageable
BUT THEN,
3. we are finally seated and instantly affronted with a very busy waiter, who proceeds to give us 3 very paltry menus, and then rattles off probably 30 “specials“ but as he’s rattling off all those specials, he’s NOT rattling off the prices - no clue on pricing
4. tried to order appetizers (since we were seated an hour later than we had anticipated, the couple I was with was hungry) but i was told, as the waiter huffed off “not placing any orders until you order your entrée”.
6. later a busboy whisked away my friends appetizer plate and fork out of her hand. She was not done eating.
7. we asked for our leftovers to go, but the beef carpaccio that was left (1/2 of the order) didn’t manage to make it into the box with the leftover pasta.
8. in the end, we had three appetizers, two entrées, a glass of champagne, a martini, a glass of wine, a port, and two desserts.
9. The bill with tip came to $420+/- which I paid with my AMEX at 10:22 PM, and which I also retained a customer copy receipt of thankfully. so imagine my surprise…
10. ACCUSED OF NOT PAYING - when at 11:45PM I get a phone call accusing me of leaving without paying my bill. I did have two martinis so in the back of my head I’m thinking anything is possible… So I checked my Amex card and sure enough it showed the charge pending and I had a copy of my receipt that was printed out from the processing machine.

I called the restaurant back and explained the situation and they said they would check into it and then they called ME back and told me I paid the WRONG bill….In a way that made it sound like it was my fault that I paid the wrong Bill, not that they gave me the wrong bill and i paid it accordingly.  (My bill was actually $60 less than the bill I paid). I was asked to come back in so that they could credit the wrong Bill an recharge me the correct amount. I told them I would be in the next day, they wanted to know what time and I said I didn’t know. The next day a woman called me and asked me what time I would be in or if they could take my credit card information over the phone…at This point I am not comfortable giving my credit card information to the restaurant over the phone so I told her I would be in later; the woman aggressively asked me if I could be in “now” and I told her no I was working and she said can you be in within an hour and I said no I’m working and she said “when can you be in” and I said between 4 o’clock and 5 o’clock.  none of this exchange is  friendly or apologetic on the Restaurants part.  I did get to the restaurant later that afternoon, and they did process the credit for the $60…i received a hug for my troubles but even that felt disingenuous.

I was hoping for a neighborhood, Italian restaurant experience at a high end, but I ended up walking away, feeling like a Timesquare tourist to be taken advantage of. I would not go back, nor would I recommend dining at this restaurant.

I understand that they were left with one ticket unpaid, but who calls a customer at 11:45 at night to accuse them of not paying their bill without making sure it isn’t their mistake first - 🤷🏻‍♀️', 'time': 1696098997, 'translated': False}, {'author_name': 'eunah k', 'author_url': 'https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/102151018826575328103/reviews', 'language': 'en', 'original_language': 'en', 'profile_photo_url': 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJNdx7ASXYU4WzeoQudC-dcAUQPDNbe5nFgo8qTvLP8RzeEHA=s128-c0x00000000-cc-rp-mo-ba4', 'rating': 3, 'relative_time_description': 'a year ago', 'text': 'Food was good. It’s more difficult to cook delicious food with simple cooking method or no sauce .because there is no way to fake it. In this respect, Dover sole was great! And I expected to be higher price since many reviews complain about over price. Well.. price was fine ,compared to other restaurants in NYC. At least here , taste is good.
